title: M/C Journal 'twitter' Issue Now Out! | Snurblog - Axel Bruns
snippet: Fabio Giglietto is mentioned as an author in the 'twitter' issue of M/C Journal. He, along with Cornelius Puschmann, discusses new requirements for public-interest research data access, particularly those implemented under the EU's Digital Services Act, and explores alternative methods for gathering data on platform activities.
description: This blog post from Snurblog mentions Fabio Giglietto as an author in the M/C Journal's 'twitter' issue, where he co-authored an article discussing data access requirements under the EU's Digital Services Act and methods for platform data gathering.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: This article references a study by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino, which found that Facebook's algorithms favor extremist, anti-European, and conspiracy theory content, while penalizing the visibility of institutional political discourse. The research analyzed over 2.5 million posts.
description: This article from lumsanews.it discusses a recent study by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino, which found that Facebook's algorithms promote extremist and conspiracy theory content, impacting elections.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme - Open
snippet: This article reports on research by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino, published on January 7, 2026. The study analyzes over 2.5 million Facebook posts and concludes that Meta's algorithmic choices have led to a decrease in visibility for institutional political content, while extremist and anti-European content has gained prominence.
description: An article reporting on Professor Fabio Giglietto's research from the University of Urbino, analyzing Facebook algorithms' impact on political content visibility, specifically regarding extremist and institutional posts.

title: Researchers meet to discuss DSA access to publicly available platform data
snippet: Fabio Giglietto is listed as one of the experts collaborating with ECAT and DG Connect in a working group to study systemic risks using platform data made accessible through the Digital Services Act (DSA). The group met on November 13, 2025, and is expected to report on their research outcomes in 2026.
description: Fabio Giglietto is listed as an expert collaborating with ECAT and DG Connect in a working group to study systemic risks using platform data made accessible through the Digital Services Act (DSA). The content discusses a recent meeting of this group held on November 13, 2025.
